project: Bike Garage - Phase 3 Persistence and Accounts
overview: Add user authentication, a database, proper image storage, and a 'Garage' concept where users can manage multiple bike profiles and access their full analysis history. Deploy the app to Vercel.
goals: Allow users to create accounts and log in
Persist analyses and maintenance history per bike
Support multiple bike profiles per user (the 'Garage')
Migrate image storage from temp files to a proper storage solution
Deploy the full app to Vercel
nonGoals: No social features (sharing, following other users)
No shop/business multi-tenancy or customer management
No mobile native app — web only
successMetrics: Users can register, log in, and log out successfully
A saved analysis is retrievable after the browser is closed and reopened
Users can manage 2+ bike profiles from their Garage
App is live and functional on Vercel with a public URL
openQuestions: What image storage provider should be used on Vercel? (Vercel Blob, Cloudinary, S3 — default: Vercel Blob)
Should analyses be shareable via a public link in a future phase?
Password reset / forgot password flow — in scope for Phase 3 or deferred?

stack.hosting: Vercel (backend as Vercel Serverless Functions or a separate Node service)
stack.database: PostgreSQL via Supabase (managed, free tier) or SQLite via better-sqlite3 for local dev
stack.auth: JWT-based auth — bcrypt for password hashing, jsonwebtoken for tokens, tokens stored in httpOnly cookies
stack.imageStorage: Vercel Blob (or equivalent) for persistent image storage; multer temp storage retained for local dev
stack.orm: Prisma (schema-first, works with Postgres and SQLite)

routes.name: Home
routes.purpose: Landing page — sign up CTA for logged-out users, redirect to /garage for logged-in users

routes.name: My Garage
routes.purpose: Grid of user's bike profiles with add new bike option

routes.name: Bike Profile
routes.purpose: Single bike page: photo, metadata, analysis history list

routes.name: Analysis Detail
routes.purpose: Full analysis view (Phase 1+2 results UI) for a saved analysis

routes.name: Upload / Analyze
routes.purpose: Photo upload form — prompts logged-in user to pick or create a bike profile to save to

routes.name: Results (transient)
routes.purpose: Immediate analysis results — user prompted to save to a bike profile

uiNotes: Garage page: card grid layout, each card shows bike name/photo thumbnail, last analyzed date, overall condition badge
Add bike card: dashed border 'Add a Bike' prompt
Bike Profile page: hero photo, bike metadata, chronological list of analyses (date, overall condition, parts count)
Analysis Detail page: same interactive UI as Phase 2 results page, read-only (no reanalysis from history view)
Upload flow update: after analysis completes, show 'Save to Garage' prompt with bike selector (existing or new)
Auth pages: minimal centered card, email + password fields, submit button, link to toggle between login/register
Global nav: logo left, 'My Garage' link, user avatar/name dropdown (logout) when logged in; Login/Register links when logged out
Protected routes redirect to /login if no valid session
rules: All protected API routes require a valid JWT in the Authorization header or httpOnly cookie
Users can only access their own bikes, analyses, and parts — no cross-user data access
Passwords must be at least 8 characters; hashed with bcrypt (cost factor 12)
JWT expiry: 7 days; no refresh token in Phase 3 (acceptable for MVP)
On save, image is uploaded to Vercel Blob and the returned URL stored in Analysis.imageUrl
Temp local file is deleted after successful upload to Vercel Blob
A Bike can have many Analyses; deleting a Bike cascades to delete all its Analyses and Parts
Saving an analysis to a bike also updates the Bike's coverImageUrl if the bike has no cover yet
Pricing results are saved at analysis time; they are not re-scraped when viewing history

stories.title: [Setup] Install Phase 3 dependencies and configure Prisma
stories.description: As a developer, I want Prisma ORM and auth dependencies installed and configured so the database schema can be defined and migrations run.
acceptanceCriteria: Install in /server: `npm install prisma @prisma/client bcrypt jsonwebtoken cookie-parser`
Install dev dependency: `npm install --save-dev @types/bcrypt @types/jsonwebtoken`
Run `npx prisma init` to create prisma/schema.prisma and .env DATABASE_URL entry
schema.prisma datasource configured for postgresql (Supabase) with fallback sqlite for local dev via DATABASE_URL env var
server/.env.example updated with: DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN
Install Vercel Blob in /server: `npm install @vercel/blob`
README updated with: how to set DATABASE_URL, how to run `npx prisma migrate dev`, how to run `npx prisma studio`
Example: running `npx prisma migrate dev --name init` with a valid DATABASE_URL succeeds
Negative case: missing DATABASE_URL → `npx prisma migrate dev` fails with clear error, not a silent hang

stories.title: Define database schema and run initial migration
stories.description: As a developer, I want the full database schema defined in Prisma and migrated so all entities are ready to use.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma defines models: User, Bike, Analysis, Part, PricingResult with all fields from the data model section
Relations: User hasMany Bike, Bike hasMany Analysis, Analysis hasMany Part, Part hasMany PricingResult
Cascade deletes configured: deleting Bike deletes Analyses; deleting Analysis deletes Parts; deleting Part deletes PricingResults
All id fields are cuid() or uuid() auto-generated
createdAt and updatedAt fields use @default(now()) and @updatedAt where applicable
boundingBox stored as Json type in Prisma schema
rawClaudeJson stored as String (TEXT) type
Migration created and applied: `npx prisma migrate dev --name init`
npm test includes a test that Prisma client can connect to the database and query User count without error
Example: after migration, `npx prisma studio` shows all 5 tables populated with correct columns
Negative case: schema with a missing required field → `prisma migrate dev` fails with a descriptive error before touching the DB

stories.title: User registration and login API endpoints
stories.description: As a new user, I want to create an account and log in so I can save my bike analyses.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/register accepts { email, password } — creates user, returns JWT in httpOnly cookie + { user: { id, email } }
POST /api/auth/login accepts { email, password } — validates credentials, returns JWT in httpOnly cookie + { user: { id, email } }
POST /api/auth/logout clears the auth cookie and returns 200
GET /api/auth/me returns { user: { id, email } } if authenticated, 401 if not
Password hashed with bcrypt cost factor 12 before storing
JWT signed with JWT_SECRET env var, expires in 7 days
Registration returns 409 if email already exists
Registration returns 400 if password < 8 characters
Login returns 401 for invalid credentials (do not reveal whether email exists)
npm test includes tests for: successful register, duplicate email 409, successful login, invalid password 401, GET /api/auth/me with valid token
Example: POST /api/auth/register with new email → 201 with user object and cookie set
Negative case: POST /api/auth/login with wrong password → 401 'Invalid credentials' (no stack trace exposed)

stories.title: Auth middleware for protected routes
stories.description: As the backend, I want an auth middleware that protects routes requiring a logged-in user.
acceptanceCriteria: requireAuth middleware reads JWT from httpOnly cookie (or Authorization: Bearer header as fallback)
Valid JWT → decodes payload, attaches { userId, email } to req.user, calls next()
Missing or invalid JWT → returns 401 { error: 'Unauthorized' }
Expired JWT → returns 401 { error: 'Session expired, please log in again' }
requireAuth is applied to all /api/garage, /api/bikes, /api/analyses routes
npm test includes tests for: request with valid token passes through, request with no token returns 401, request with expired token returns 401
Example: GET /api/garage with valid cookie → req.user populated, handler proceeds
Negative case: GET /api/garage with tampered JWT signature → 401 Unauthorized

stories.title: Bike profile CRUD API
stories.description: As a user, I want to create, view, update, and delete bike profiles in my garage so I can manage my collection.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/bikes — create a new bike: { name, brand, model, type, year, color, frame_material, notes } → 201 with bike object
GET /api/bikes — list all bikes for authenticated user → 200 with array of bike objects (newest first)
GET /api/bikes/:bikeId — get single bike with analyses summary (id, createdAt, overallCondition, parts count) → 200 or 404
PATCH /api/bikes/:bikeId — update bike metadata fields → 200 with updated bike
DELETE /api/bikes/:bikeId — delete bike and all cascaded analyses → 204
All endpoints enforce ownership: user can only access their own bikes (return 404 for other users' bikes, not 403)
name field is required for bike creation; all other fields optional
npm test includes tests for: create bike, list bikes (empty and populated), get bike, update bike, delete bike, attempt to access another user's bike → 404
Example: POST /api/bikes { name: 'Trek Road Bike', brand: 'Trek' } → 201 with id and createdAt
Negative case: DELETE /api/bikes/:otherUsersBikeId → 404 (not 403, to avoid revealing existence)

stories.title: Save analysis to database with image upload to Vercel Blob
stories.description: As a user, I want to save an analysis result to a specific bike profile so I can access it later.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/bikes/:bikeId/analyses accepts the Claude analysis JSON + pricing results in the request body
Image file is uploaded to Vercel Blob using BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN; returned URL stored in Analysis.imageUrl
Temp local file deleted after successful Blob upload
Analysis record created in DB with: bikeId, userId, imageUrl, overallCondition, summary, rawClaudeJson
Each part in the analysis creates a Part record linked to the analysis
Each pricing result for each part creates a PricingResult record linked to the part
If the bike has no coverImageUrl yet, set it to the analysis imageUrl
Returns 201 with { analysisId } on success
Returns 404 if bikeId doesn't belong to the authenticated user
npm test includes tests for: successful save returns 201 with analysisId, save to non-owned bike returns 404
Example: POST /api/bikes/abc123/analyses with full analysis payload → 201 { analysisId: 'xyz789' }
Negative case: Vercel Blob upload fails → return 502, do not create a partial DB record

stories.title: Analysis history retrieval API
stories.description: As a user, I want to retrieve past analyses for a bike so I can track its maintenance history over time.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/bikes/:bikeId/analyses — list all analyses for a bike (newest first): [{ id, createdAt, overallCondition, summary, partsCount }]
GET /api/bikes/:bikeId/analyses/:analysisId — full analysis detail including all parts and pricing results
Both endpoints enforce ownership (return 404 for other users' bikes/analyses)
Full analysis response shape matches the same structure as the Phase 1 POST /api/analyze response so the frontend Results page can render it without changes
npm test includes tests for: list analyses (empty and populated), get full analysis detail, access control
Example: GET /api/bikes/abc123/analyses → array of 3 analyses with createdAt dates and condition
Negative case: GET /api/bikes/abc123/analyses/nonexistent → 404

stories.title: Frontend auth flow — register, login, logout, protected routes
stories.description: As a user, I want to register, log in, and log out from the frontend with protected routes that redirect to login when unauthenticated.
acceptanceCriteria: RegisterPage at /register: email + password fields, submit calls POST /api/auth/register, redirects to /garage on success
LoginPage at /login: email + password fields, submit calls POST /api/auth/login, redirects to /garage on success
AuthContext (React context) provides { user, login, logout, isLoading } to the whole app
On app load, GET /api/auth/me is called to restore session; isLoading=true until response received
Logout calls POST /api/auth/logout and clears user from context, redirects to /login
ProtectedRoute wrapper component: redirects to /login if user is null and isLoading is false
/garage, /garage/:bikeId, /upload wrapped in ProtectedRoute
Global nav renders Login/Register links when logged out; user email + Logout when logged in
npm run lint passes
Example: visit /garage while logged out → redirected to /login → login → redirected back to /garage
Negative case: register with already-used email → inline error 'An account with this email already exists'

stories.title: Garage page — bike profile grid
stories.description: As a user, I want to see all my bike profiles on the Garage page so I can quickly navigate to any bike.
acceptanceCriteria: GaragePage at /garage fetches GET /api/bikes and displays bikes as a responsive card grid
Each bike card shows: cover image (or a placeholder icon), bike name, brand+model, last analyzed date (from most recent analysis), overall condition badge
Clicking a bike card navigates to /garage/:bikeId
An 'Add a Bike' card (dashed border) navigates to a simple create-bike modal or inline form
Create bike form: name (required), brand, model, type dropdown, year, color, frame_material, notes — submit calls POST /api/bikes
New bike appears in the grid without a full page reload
Empty state: 'Your garage is empty — add your first bike!' with a prominent Add button
npm run lint passes
Example: garage with 2 bikes → 2 cards + 1 add card rendered in grid
Negative case: GET /api/bikes fails with 500 → show 'Failed to load your garage. Please refresh.' error state

stories.title: Bike profile page with analysis history
stories.description: As a user, I want to view a single bike's profile page with its photo, metadata, and a history of all its analyses so I can track changes over time.
acceptanceCriteria: BikeProfilePage at /garage/:bikeId fetches GET /api/bikes/:bikeId and GET /api/bikes/:bikeId/analyses
Hero section: bike cover photo, bike name, metadata (brand, model, type, year, color, frame_material)
Edit button opens an inline form to PATCH bike metadata
Delete button shows a confirmation dialog, then calls DELETE /api/bikes/:bikeId and redirects to /garage
Analysis history section: chronological list (newest first) with date, overall condition badge, summary snippet, parts count
Clicking an analysis entry navigates to /garage/:bikeId/analyses/:analysisId
Empty analysis history: 'No analyses yet — analyze this bike to start tracking maintenance'
npm run lint passes
Example: bike with 3 analyses → 3 entries shown newest first with dates and condition badges
Negative case: navigate to /garage/nonexistent → 404 page with 'Bike not found' and link back to /garage

stories.title: Analysis detail page — view saved analysis with Phase 2 interactive UI
stories.description: As a user, I want to view a previously saved analysis with the full Phase 2 interactive UI so I can review part conditions and pricing as of that analysis date.
acceptanceCriteria: AnalysisDetailPage at /garage/:bikeId/analyses/:analysisId fetches GET /api/bikes/:bikeId/analyses/:analysisId
Renders the same Results UI as Phase 2: bike photo with part overlays, detail panel, sortable parts table with pricing
Reanalyze button is NOT shown on history view (read-only)
Page title shows the analysis date: 'Analysis from March 12, 2026'
Back breadcrumb: '← Back to [Bike Name]' navigates to /garage/:bikeId
Results UI reuses Phase 2 components without duplication — components accept data as props, not from router state
npm run lint passes
Example: navigate to saved analysis → full interactive results UI loads with photo overlays and historical pricing
Negative case: fetch fails → error state with 'Could not load this analysis' and retry button

stories.title: Save to Garage flow — prompt user to save after analysis
stories.description: As a logged-in user, I want to be prompted to save my analysis results to a bike profile immediately after analysis completes so my data isn't lost.
acceptanceCriteria: After POST /api/analyze returns on the upload/results flow, a 'Save to Garage' banner appears at the top of the Results page
Banner contains a bike selector dropdown (populated from GET /api/bikes) and a 'Save' button
Option to create a new bike inline from the selector (opens a minimal create-bike form)
Clicking Save calls POST /api/bikes/:bikeId/analyses with the current analysis data
On success: banner updates to 'Saved! View in Garage →' with a link to the bike profile
On failure: banner shows 'Save failed. Try again.'
If user is not logged in, banner shows 'Log in to save this analysis to your Garage' with a Login link
npm run lint passes
Example: logged-in user completes analysis → banner shows → selects 'Trek Road Bike' → clicks Save → success message with link
Negative case: user not logged in → banner shows login prompt, Save button not visible

stories.title: Vercel deployment — frontend and backend
stories.description: As a developer, I want the app deployed to Vercel with all environment variables configured so users can access it publicly.
acceptanceCriteria: vercel.json created at project root configuring: frontend (React/Vite) as static output, backend (Express) as a Vercel Serverless Function or rewrites to /api/*
Alternative: backend deployed as a separate Vercel project if Express-as-serverless is not viable
Vercel project environment variables set: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL (Supabase prod), JWT_SECRET, BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN, CLIENT_URL
Prisma migration run against production Supabase database before first deploy
npm run build succeeds for both client and server without errors
Production deploy accessible at a public Vercel URL
Full end-to-end smoke test on production: register → add bike → upload photo → analyze → save to garage → view history
Example: visiting the Vercel URL → app loads, register works, analysis flow works end-to-end
Negative case: missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in Vercel env vars → /api/analyze returns 500 with 'Configuration error' (not the raw key error)
